calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Determining Distribution Rights, And For Order Allowing Payment Of Attorney Fees.
Off Calendar Aug-30-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Otonello for Linda Harrington.

No other Parties were present. Mr. Otonello agreed with the Court that Mr. Ross has not met the burden of proof to establish his entitlement to a share of Essie's share of the Trust. The issue of fees was taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Dec-04-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Otonello for Linda Harrington. Bruce Feder for the Conservatee. Mozell Brooks intrested party also present.

Mr. Otonello stated that in addition to the management of the trust assets, that Ms. Harrington provided personal service to the Conservatee for years, that she assisted with medical issues and with placement. In addition, he provided an explanation and breakown of the late payment charges. Mr. Feder stated that Ms. Harrington did take care of the Conservatee, that he had no disagreement with the Examiner's observation and that it was justified for the Court to adjust Ms. Harrington's fees. The Court stated that there would be a reduction in fees and took the matter under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (810 Faxon Avenue, San Francisco)
Off Calendar Sep-15-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Otonello for Linda Harrington.

The correct property address is 801 Faxon Avenue, San Francisco, CA. The sale was confirmed at $675,000.00. An additional bond in the amount of $161,000.00 is required. The Court granted the petition (signing the order for the same in open court).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley CSR 225. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition For Authority To Sell Personal Residence And To Execute Exclusive Listing Agreement
Off Calendar Jun-02-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Ottonello for and with Linda Harrington (Trustee). Mary Sancimino for Roza Lending (Lender). Bruce Feder for Mattie McGowan (Beneficiary).

Mr. Ottonello stated that he had looked into a RAM and that they stop lending at $625,000 even if the house is worth more. Mr. Feder stated that his client would like to stay in her home, that she is not steady on her feet, that if she were to go to a skilled nursing facility and spend down her money she would prefer to stay in her home 10 or 12 months, and then she may want to go to a board and care. Ms. Harrington stated that there is a vacancy in the office of Conservator of the Person, that she doesn't have authority to place her, that per Ms. McGowan's niece and the new Proposed Successor Conservator she may be placed with the niece if modifications could be done to her home, that there is a new petition on file for the appointment of a Conservator, and that the property can't be shown before Ms. McGowan is placed. In addition, she stated that she has been working with the family a long time, that a skilled nursing facility won't take Medi-Cal, that she would need 6 to 12 months of funds before the skilled facility would take her, and then she would have to be placed out of San Francisco. Ms. Mozelle Brooks stated that Ms. McGowan has worked hard, that she wants to remain in her home, and that she might harm herself if she has to leave her home. The Court stated that there would only be enough money for an additional year in her home. The Court granted the petition, (signing the order for the same in open court). Mr. Ottonello stated that they had reached an agreement as tot he extension of the sale, notice of default, etc., as set forth on the record.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Order To Appear
Off Calendar Apr-29-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for Mattie McGowan (Conservatee). Dennis Vann for Willa Johnson. Ed Otonello for and with Linda Harrington.

The Court noted that Mary Sancimino was not present. Mr. Otonello confirmed that the property located at 801 Faxon was in foreclosure, stated that the default notice had been recorded on March 25, 2009 and that they had 90 days to cure the foreclosure. In addition, he stated that the amount owed was $250,000.00 plus additional fees, that there was a large care bill, that the fair market value of the house was estimated at $800,000.00 and that the plan was to sell the house, pay the care expenses and use the remainder for the Conservatee's living expenses. The Court signed an Order Shortening Time For Hearing Of The Petition For Authority To Sell The Property which is to be heard on June 2, 2009. The Court also signed an Order Directing Transfer Of Account To Trustee for the U.S. Bank account. Mr. Vann stated that Fanny Reuben had not been involved for about two years, that Willa Johnson was willing to be appointed Conservator of the person and the petition was to be heard on June 11, 2009.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Arnold.
